rows.title: strict manifest and six content kinds
closed/reopened PWA offline starter run with persisted saves/settings
rollback/quota/migration/offline store behavior and save portability
original responsive landing and three CTAs
browser/device QA including save export/import and restore
public npm packages and CLI clean-room use
Cloudflare Pages site plus GitHub Pages fallback

defined.expectedEvidence: `package.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `.npmrc`, `tsconfig*.json`, and private workspace package metadata are present; `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` and `pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit` complete in the future scaffold without creating public packages.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if the root is not private, the lock is not reproducible, or dependency pins were copied from the old reference project instead of resolved.

defined.expectedEvidence: `.github/workflows/*.yml`, CODEOWNERS, dependency-review, CodeQL, secret-scan, lockfile, types, unit, build, content-policy, license-provenance, package-pack, and e2e-offline jobs are defined with pinned action SHAs and least privilege.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if required checks are missing, unpinned, overprivileged, or cannot run on a fork-safe preview artifact.

defined.expectedEvidence: `packages/protocol/src/**`, generated JSON Schema, and adversarial fixtures prove strict unknown-field rejection for level, character, object-pack, campaign, music, and bundle manifests.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if the browser cannot share the same validator/types or if any v1 capability permits executable content.

defined.expectedEvidence: Threat tests and catalog policy reject JS, WASM, HTML, CSS, lifecycle scripts, dynamic `import()`, `eval`, undeclared URLs, tarball handling, and browser-side npm resolution.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if any community package can execute code in the browser or bypass curated exact-version catalog review.

defined.expectedEvidence: Threat model and provenance policy cover traversal, symlink, Unicode/case collision, MIME, hash, semver, license, GLB external/data/network URI, extraction, and compression-ratio attacks.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if a rejection rule lacks a test fixture or if provenance cannot identify reviewer, date, source, license, and hashes.

defined.expectedEvidence: Installer protocol spec defines planned -> staging -> verifying -> committing -> installed/failed/canceled states, transaction invariants, exact DAG resolution, preflight quota, and failure cleanup.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if P07 would need to invent transaction semantics or if previous locks could be destroyed by a failed install.

defined.expectedEvidence: Architecture docs state v1 has no backend/account/cloud save/leaderboard/multiplayer; later D1 requires online story, auth, abuse, privacy, retention, export/deletion, migration, quota/cost, backup/restore, and load-test ADR.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if any Phase 01-11 task depends on Supabase, D1, accounts, or remote user data for v1 completion.

defined.expectedEvidence: `content/starter-*`, asset pipeline scripts, source hashes, GLB validation logs, budget reports, and reference renders show exact-artifact-cleared Quaternius, KayKit, or Kenney CC0 content only.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if Pelican or OS3A is treated as verified without original creator/source/license proof, or if runtime packs require generated React modules.

defined.expectedEvidence: Machine-readable provenance and human ledger include assetId, package/version/path, role, MIME, bytes, SHA-256, creator/source/acquisition, source artifact hash, SPDX/license URL/hash, attribution, modifications, recipe, output hash, reviewer/date, and evidence status.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if any runtime asset lacks provenance, a captured license, or a withdrawal/replacement path.

defined.expectedEvidence: Starter music evidence shows commissioned/original, CC0, or CC-BY tracks with attribution; policy rejects NC, ND, ambiguous royalty-free grants, and the linked Katamari soundtrack.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if any track exceeds 8 MiB/10 minutes, pack exceeds 32 MiB/8 tracks, or unverified copyrighted soundtrack material is bundled, mirrored, streamed, suggested, cataloged, or auto-installed.

defined.expectedEvidence: Future-local-import policy keeps bytes, filenames, tags, artwork, waveforms, hashes, playlists, and rights assertions local-only and out of catalog, network, analytics, diagnostics, cloud/export, screenshots, and service-worker requests.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if local user audio becomes a vertical-slice blocker or any design identifies famous works or implies rights.

defined.expectedEvidence: Engine/gameplay/runtime-r3f tests prove tag/AoS Koota with `Function` blocked as a JS dynamic-code guard, stable-ID ordered systems/lifecycle, one paused raw Rapier step/event queue, physics-only proxies, plain poses, and one visible-transform writer. P04 explicitly hands off the pinned-WASM CSP/MIME browser gate to P05/P10/P11 rather than claiming full CSP coverage.
defined.stopCondition: Stop on JS `unsafe-eval`, SoA accessors, implicit query order, duplicate clocks/event queues/transform writers, visible physics children, or Ecctrl snowball authority; do not block required pinned Rapier WASM accidentally.

defined.expectedEvidence: Installed-API contract and adapter tests prove Ecctrl 2.0.0 `setMovement` receives complete normalized state including releases, stays optional for humanoid/on-foot/touch modes, and never owns snowball physics or deterministic stepping.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if the snowball depends on an Ecctrl capsule/frame loop, incomplete sticky movement state, or undocumented/deep Ecctrl imports.

defined.expectedEvidence: `packages/input/**` tests cover movement plus semantic `up/down/left/right/pause/confirm/back`, keyboard normalization/no repeat edges, gamepad movement deadzone and UI direction activation/release hysteresis, touch direct activation/pointer cancel, one edge per transition, arbitration, and synthetic releases.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if UI/gameplay reads raw devices, focus navigation steals gameplay source, or device/browser repeat produces duplicate edges.

defined.expectedEvidence: Telemetry harness records frame p95, draw calls, visible triangles, active bodies, heap, long tasks, input-to-simulation, physics p95, and worker-transfer traces before any OffscreenCanvas decision.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if workers are justified without two traces showing at least 20% frame-budget recovery and transfer overhead below 25% of saved time.

defined.expectedEvidence: 90-second one-arena run proves three authored size bands, final goal object, authored integer score, win/time-out, pause, restart on same level version/seed, growth volume formula, collection threshold, camera reset/options, and collected-body disappearance by next tick.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if four of five fresh playtesters cannot finish within three attempts or median fun/control is below 4/5.

defined.expectedEvidence: Keyboard, standard gamepad, and touch runs complete the same starter objective with equivalent pause/restart/camera/action behavior and no P0/P1 gameplay, input parity, accessibility, or offline defects.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if any input source is required to finish but cannot, or if pause/confirm/back conflicts with source arbitration.

defined.expectedEvidence: One checksum-identical P05 artifact passes isolated secure-context root/project-prefix checks for exact name/short_name/id/play-start/scope/standalone, absent `prefer_related_applications`, real response-backed 192/512 PNG purpose-any icons, prefix-correct fetch-capable SW/controller, exact starter precache, Dexie v1 saves/settings, fresh persistent-profile offline completion, and Chromium browser-native installability signaling. This is automated manifest/SW/cache/profile evidence, not an OS-installed-app lifecycle; P10 owns headed/manual real install -> full app/browser quit -> network-off relaunch on supported shipping platforms and explicit unsupported rows elsewhere.
defined.stopCondition: Stop on any required manifest field/absence/icon declaration or response mismatch, host-specific bytes/rewrite, prefix/worker/cache/save/offline divergence, catalog/store dependency leakage, failed Chromium signal, or any claim that Playwright reopening proves OS installation.

defined.expectedEvidence: The real production artifact embeds the approved meta CSP baseline only for the Chromium P05 proof (no CSP response header), boots `/play`, loads and steps pinned same-origin Rapier WASM under `script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'` with no JS `'unsafe-eval'`/inline script, and serves `.wasm` as `application/wasm`. P05 owns a build-emitted inert same-origin external probe asset at a recorded artifact path with a SHA-256 hash; the allowed external probe records the document-realm `new Function` thrown error plus `securitypolicyviolation`, and a separately inserted nonce-less inline script never sets its marker and emits a violation. Handoff records release-artifact, effective policy, probe asset, and WASM hashes and proves no community WASM is emitted or fetched.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if an automation-world `page.evaluate(new Function)` is used as proof, a CSP header is used for the P05 Chromium gate, the probe is inline or un-hashed, Rapier falls back from bad MIME, JS dynamic code is allowed, community WASM is emitted/fetched, or the Koota shim is presented as complete CSP proof.

defined.expectedEvidence: `packages/cli/**` exposes `npx infinite-snowball` and `pnpm dlx infinite-snowball` commands `init`, `validate --strict --json`, `convert`, `build`, `preview`, `pack`, authoring `install <exact-spec>`, `catalog verify`, `submit`, and `publish --dry-run` with a real-publish gate.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if the CLI runs lifecycle scripts for community content or publishes without explicit external npm gate approval.

defined.expectedEvidence: Templates, examples, docs snippets, fixtures, and validation errors let an author create, validate, preview, pack, and dry-run submit a data-only package with exact dependencies and budget/licensing feedback.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if examples require private credentials, arbitrary JS, or ambiguous licenses.

defined.expectedEvidence: Catalog submission fixtures and CI contract record npm provenance, exact version/integrity, per-file hashes, reviewer/date/evidence, budgets, licenses, withdrawal/replacement, and maintainer review status.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if the lifecycle silently accepts unreviewed npm search results or lacks transparent rejection reasons.

defined.expectedEvidence: Dexie tables `catalogSnapshots`, `catalogEntries`, `packages`, `assets`, `packageAssets`, `packageLocks`, `installTransactions`, `saves`, `settings`, and `migrations` rehydrate the last valid catalog instantly while background refresh preserves visible data; `catalogEntries` and `packages` are distinct normalized tables.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if refresh blanks the store, loses last-valid data, merges catalog-entry/package identities, or bypasses generated protocol validation.

defined.expectedEvidence: Cache Storage, Dexie, and controlled `apps/web/src/sw.ts` extension evidence shows exact acyclic DAG resolution, quota/persistence gesture flow, streamed hash/type/size verification, immutable cache writes, reference counts, active pointer commit, side-by-side updates, and offline install only for already verified cached objects while preserving the P05 starter precache.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if install commits before every file verifies, if uninstall removes a file still referenced by another package, or if the service-worker change replaces the P05 baseline instead of extending it.

defined.expectedEvidence: Failure/cancel/migration/quota/save-portability tests show staging-byte deletion only, retained failed/canceled transaction rows with error/verified set/rollback actions/reconciliation status, prior lock preservation, atomic/idempotent migration of P05 Dexie v1 `saves`/`settings`, cache/DB reconciliation, one retained known-good shell, versioned SaveExport round-trip, corrupt/oversize/incompatible import fail-closed behavior, rollback, privacy/non-egress checks, and a rerun of `VG-05-OFFLINE-RUN` after P07 service-worker/catalog changes.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if an update can destroy previous usable data, storage persistence is promised instead of observed, failed transaction audit evidence is deleted, save import/export can leak private/local-audio data, or the P05 starter offline regression fails.

defined.expectedEvidence: Splash route shows original logo, snow-paper/cut-paper/toy-block identity, semantic Press Start button, disabled boot progress until ready, Enter/Space/gamepad Confirm/tap support, audio gesture unlock, explicit offline starter readiness, and Retry on fatal boot.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if identity copies trade dress or if splash is non-semantic or keyboard/gamepad inaccessible.

defined.expectedEvidence: HUD, pause, store, and Settings Save Data states render from throttled snapshots/view models; pause stops simulation and traps/restores focus; store shows cached-refresh, offline timestamp, no-cache, error, quota, downloading, verifying, installed, update, canceled, and failed states; Save Data export/import gives accessible success, error, and recovery copy.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if DOM UI reads raw input/IndexedDB APIs, if pause only hides UI while simulation keeps ticking, or if save import/export feedback is ambiguous or inaccessible.

defined.expectedEvidence: Landing includes the truthful required IA/CTAs plus paired pre/post proof that only start/description/theme/background changed while exact name/short_name/standalone/id/scope, absent `prefer_related_applications`, real 192/512 PNG purpose-any icons, icon responses, and SW behavior persisted.
defined.stopCondition: Stop on misleading/trade-dress/unverified UI or any unauthorized manifest/icon/SW change.

defined.expectedEvidence: Responsive and accessibility evidence covers 375, 768, 1024, 1440, 375 landscape-safe gameplay, WCAG 2.2 AA contrast, >=2px focus, semantic names/states/live regions, accessible Save Data controls/messages, no color-only state, >=44x44 targets, no >3 flashes/sec, and reduced motion <=100ms fades without parallax/rolling decoration/count-ups/large transforms.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if a P0/P1 accessibility, responsive, motion, save-control, or touch-target defect remains.

defined.expectedEvidence: Mintlify source implements Start Here, Create Packs, Reference, Contribute, and Project IA; player, creator, and contributor journeys each reach prerequisites, expected result, recovery, and next step in <=3 navigation choices.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if docs depend on hosted-only content not stored in the repo or contradict protocol/security/licensing contracts.

defined.expectedEvidence: Contributor contracts, policies, issue/PR templates, examples, code/content review requirements, license/provenance instructions, and community guidance align with no-code content, curated catalog, and original identity.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if contribution docs invite arbitrary browser JS, unverified assets, backend requirements, or soundtrack redistribution.

defined.expectedEvidence: README includes truthful screenshots, badges, instructions, local development, CLI/package workflow, offline/store behavior, accessibility, security/provenance, no-backend-v1, deployment notes, and exact unsupported external gates.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if README claims live deployment, badges, screenshots, npm, or account setup before evidence exists.

defined.expectedEvidence: Screenshot manifest maps real captured assets to routes, viewport/device, commit SHA, capture command, alt text, and allowed README/docs placement.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if any screenshot is mocked, stale, untraceable, or implies unavailable app-store/native support.

defined.expectedEvidence: Cross-engine/device QA first defines and freezes a matrix schema, then executes automated Chromium, Firefox, Playwright-WebKit, `mobile-chromium`, and `mobile-webkit` rows against the latest post-P08 CSP/manifest proof. Each Chromium project uses a fresh candidate/project/origin profile proving manifest/document/SW/icon/cache bytes, Playwright version, full Browser/empty installability response, and same-profile offline relaunch. Distinct manual rows validate shipping macOS Safari and real-iPhone Safari on real devices; capable shipping rows separately prove native lifecycle and unsupported rows name capability/reason.
defined.stopCondition: Stop on an unapproved/unfrozen matrix, missing latest post-P08 CSP proof, manifest/icon mismatch, stale/reused/off-record/mocked Chromium evidence, `IN_INCOGNITO`, supported lifecycle failure, missing shipping Safari/real iPhone rows, vague unsupported row, substitution, or unresolved P0/P1 defect.

defined.expectedEvidence: Performance traces for low/mobile, mid, and high tiers report p95 frame budgets, draw calls, triangles, bodies, heap, long tasks, input latency, physics p95, and three-run heap trend.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if profile data misses a tier or any initial budget fails without an explicit owner-approved fix or documented stop.

defined.expectedEvidence: Mandatory code review, security review, license/provenance review, accessibility review, matrix-schema review, and release-candidate signoff are recorded with issue links, findings, fixes, remaining risk, and explicit approval of `VG-10-DEVICES` matrix completeness before execution evidence is accepted.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if any mandatory review is skipped, self-approved without evidence, leaves unresolved release-blocking findings, or signs a matrix that lacks an owned schema/project/manual-validator definition.

defined.expectedEvidence: All external gates and previews are green; CODEOWNERS, pinned workflows, ruleset/protection, required checks, and security settings are active before visibility/tagging or preconfigured where GitHub requires visibility, then immediately reverified after conversion before tag creation.
defined.stopCondition: Stop on unresolved authority/evidence, any unprotected public window, failed post-conversion control, exact P01/final-SHA audit gap, or private/generated leak; remove/hold tag and restore reversible state.

defined.expectedEvidence: Local tarball preflight passes; inert tag and live repo/web/docs are green; then local orchestration explicitly dispatches/monitors the protected-CI trusted OIDC publisher last, with integrity/provenance/dist-tags and clean-room CLI verification.
defined.stopCondition: Stop on tag auto-trigger, local publish/token, early npm dispatch, unresolved authority, failed live gate/verification, or unusable deprecation/dist-tag restoration; deprecate/restore and rollback reversible state.

defined.expectedEvidence: Hosts use fresh `{artifact hash, project, origin, purpose: web-check}` Chromium evidence only for live delivery reruns, not the full P10 matrix. Cloudflare proves the approved CSP header and embedded meta intersect without weakening the approved policy; GitHub Pages proves the approved meta-only fallback. Both hosts bind release SHA, artifact hash, effective policy hash, P05 probe hash, and Rapier WASM hash, then pass manifest/icon/SW/CDP/offline/CSP/WASM/document-probe checks. The selected live-primary native lifecycle uses a distinct clean purpose/profile.
defined.stopCondition: Stop on host failure; stale/reused/unbound profile; missing release/policy/probe/WASM hash; Cloudflare header/meta conflict; GitHub Pages header requirement; missing purpose; cross-use; dirty native start; document/cache byte mismatch; lifecycle failure; checksum-only proof; one-host proof; or full-P10-matrix substitution.

defined.expectedEvidence: Mintlify project/domain external gate is resolved; live docs match repo source, navigation works, links pass, and release URL is recorded.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if Mintlify credentials/domain are missing or live docs diverge from repo source.

defined.expectedEvidence: After every release push, all eleven named required repository checks (`lockfile`, `types`, `unit`, `build`, `content-policy`, `license-provenance`, `package-pack`, `e2e-offline`, `dependency-review`, `codeql`, `secret-scan`) reach terminal green; no-check is allowed only for explicitly non-required surfaces; failures are fixed and re-pushed; rollback commands and bad npm deprecation/dist-tag restoration are documented.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if any required check is pending, missing, failing, or incorrectly marked no-check, or if rollback is untested/undocumented.

defined.expectedEvidence: Live README images, links, badges, screenshots, npm references, site/docs URLs, fallback URLs, and final-SHA review evidence are opened/verified after public release.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if any public-facing link, badge, image, instruction, or final-SHA review reference is stale, broken, or unsupported by live evidence.

defined.expectedEvidence: Final audit maps every primary D001-D042 deliverable row, the supplemental `VG-05-CSP-WASM` stop gate, each phase gate, external cutover gate, public artifact, and rollback path to evidence; it confirms no orphan gate references, duplicate owners/gates, forbidden scaffold/source/config, or historical/current repo-state conflation.
defined.stopCondition: Stop if any D001-D042 row or supplemental gate is missing evidence, has multiple owners/gates, is orphaned, contradicts security/licensing/offline/input/performance/UI/no-backend boundaries, or erases the historical public-repo initialization facts while reporting current state.

history.publicRepositoryLine: Historical repository fact for validators and handoffs: the public repository is <https://github.com/jeffscottward/infinite-snowball> (`PUBLIC`, default branch `main`), and only the existing Phase 01 commit was pushed when that public repository was initialized; later planning/implementation work remains local until explicitly reviewed and released.
history.publicationBoundaryLine: Historical repository fact for validators and handoffs: the public repository is <https://github.com/jeffscottward/infinite-snowball> (`PUBLIC`, default branch `main`), and only the existing Phase 01 commit was pushed when that public repository was initialized; later planning/implementation work remains local until explicitly reviewed and released.
